pattern: A subarray stays valid exactly as long as its running max and min differ by at most 2, so a sliding window backed by two monotonic deques -- shrinking only when that gap breaks -- counts every valid subarray in one linear pass.
hints: As you extend a window to the right, all that matters for validity is the current maximum and minimum inside it -- track both of those instead of comparing every pair of elements directly.

Maintain the running max and min of the current window incrementally (a monotonic decreasing deque and a monotonic increasing deque of indices both work) rather than recomputing them from scratch on every extension.

Whenever the window's max minus min exceeds 2, shrink it from the left until it's valid again; every window ending at the current right edge that starts anywhere from the left edge onward is valid, so add its length to a running total.

edge_inputs.description: statement-style example with a mix of valid and invalid windows
strictly increasing by 1, every subarray valid
all identical elements, every subarray valid
values spaced exactly 2 apart throughout, all still valid
a value 3 apart from the window breaks continuity even though neighbors look close
one outlier resets the window entirely

stress.description: 100000 random values across the full magnitude range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
